prosody.intonation: flat with rising terminal contours on non-final phrases
prosody.fluencyNotes: Generally fluent but lacks native linking and assimilation; word boundaries remain distinctly separated

strengths: Consistent pacing without unnatural mid-phrase hesitations
Clear articulation of vowels with strong projection

recommendedFocus.reason: Consistent substitution with alveolar fricatives (/s/, /z/) acts as a strong accent marker and impedes word-level recognition for minimal pairs
H-dropping significantly alters word meanings (e.g., house vs. ounce) and breaks intelligibility for native listeners
Lack of reduction in unstressed syllables prevents the natural stress-timed rhythm of English, making the speech sound disjointed

result.transcript: Yesterday the weather was really beautiful. So I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
